latest.title: Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
latest.content:
25 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9578X
IG Design Group PLC
25 March 2026
25 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, announces that its subsidiary, IG Design Group UK Limited, has completed the sale of a surplus warehouse at Hirwaun Industrial Estate, Aberdare to the Welsh Government for c.£3.1 million. As previously indicated in the February 2026 trading update, this disposal forms part of the Group's efforts to strengthen its balance sheet. The carrying value of the site was £0.1 million and the sale will realise a profit of £2.9 million (after £0.1m of selling costs and taxes).
​For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison

latest.title: Replacement: Capital Reduction Update
latest.content:
4 Mar 2026 13:20
RNS Number : 3511V
IG Design Group PLC
04 March 2026
The following amendment has been made to the Capital Reduction Update announcement released on 4 March 2026 at 12:15 under RNS No 3439V ("the Announcement").
The Announcement should have been dated 4 March 2026. This amendment has been made below.
All other details remain unchanged.
4 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Capital Reduction Update
The board of directors of IG Design Group plc is pleased to announce that the reduction of capital (the "
Capital Reduction
") required to effect the cancellation of the Capital Reduction Shares issued by the Company prior to the Court Hearing together with share premium and the capital redemption reserve was approved on 3 March 2026 by the High Court of Justice of England and Wales (the "
Court
").
The Court order confirming the Capital Reduction and a statement of capital approved by the Court in connection therewith will shortly be sent to the Registrar of Companies. The Capital Reduction will become effective upon the registration of the Court order and associated statement of capital by the Registrar of Companies. Completion of the Capital Reduction will not affect the rights attaching to the Ordinary Shares and will not result in any change to the number of Ordinary Shares in issue (or their nominal value).
The Capital Reduction will allow the Company to potentially make future shareholder distributions.
Full details of the Capital Reduction are set out in the notice of extraordinary general meeting which was sent to shareholders of the Company on 15 January 2026 (the "
EGM Circular
"). The EGM Circular is also available for inspection on the Company website,
Reports and Presentations - Design Group
. Unless otherwise stated, defined terms in this announcement are the same as those set out in the EGM Circular. As set out in the EGM Circular, provisional dates were previously obtained by the Company for the required Court Hearings, which were subsequently changed at the discretion of the Company.
For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: IG Design Group makes and sells creative party and celebration goods – from themed decorations to novelty gift items that people use for birthdays, weddings, corporate events and seasonal celebrations.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The party‑goods market is steady and growing, especially online. Brands that can design eye‑catching, cost‑effective products hold a competitive edge, and IG’s catalogue is well‑known in the UK and Ireland, giving it a ready‑made customer base.
company_30s.current_state: IG has been trimming its balance sheet – selling a surplus warehouse, cutting capital, and bringing in a new institutional backer. The company is still in a lean phase, focusing on core product lines while trying to improve cash flow. Its share price sits very low compared with the size of its assets.

rns_narrative: IG is shedding non‑core assets – the Hirwaun warehouse sale shows a move to reduce inventory and free cash.
A capital reduction and an extraordinary general meeting signal a serious restructuring of shareholder equity and a push to tighten finances.
The latest trading update and the new investor announcement suggest that the company is trying to rebuild confidence and attract fresh capital.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet chatter of a possible comeback after a deep sell‑off
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders praise the warehouse sale and the new investor as signs that the business is turning a corner.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Some voices worry that a capital cut is just a bandage – the company may still lack a clear growth plan and could keep bleeding cash.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The discussion has been very muted for weeks, but the recent bullish tweets give a slight lift, hinting that a catalyst may soon emerge.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for IGR.L is flat – no public interest, so the next news hit could surprise the market.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the warehouse sale – a clean exit from a non‑core asset that should improve liquidity.
Official confirmation of the new institutional investor’s stake – a vote of confidence that could lift the share price.
Release of the next earnings report – a positive surprise would validate the restructuring.
A volume spike when the trading update is posted – a classic sign that traders are taking notice.
Price touching the previous support level near the sale price – a potential bounce point.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sustained 2‑week volume rise, a clear statement that the capital reduction has closed, and a price break above the 0.12p support. Any of these would reinforce the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock appears to be in the early phase of recovery – capitulation seems complete, but the market is still waiting for the first signs of a rebound. A mean‑reversion bounce could start as soon as the next trading update or earnings release.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: IG’s share has plunged to a deep crash level, and the company’s recent moves (asset sale, capital cut, new investor) are typical precursors to a rebound. Historical RED‑color signals show an average return of 162 % when the price recovers to halfway between the crash and the pre‑crash peak. If the share rebounds to 0.20p from the current 0.10p, that’s a 100 % gain; a 0.30p recovery would deliver 200 %.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still faces liquidity strain; the capital reduction may not be enough to sustain operations. If the new investor pulls back or earnings disappoint, the share could slide back to zero. The worst case is a total wipe‑out of the remaining equity.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: +160 % (historical mean for a deep crash). Potential downside: –70 % (the drawdown already seen). The asymmetry is strongly favourable for a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when volume spikes after the trading update and the price closes above the 0.12p support. Size the position for 5 % of your discretionary capital and set a stop 20 % below the entry to protect against a relapse.
playbook.if_watching: Hold until the new investor’s stake is confirmed or the earnings release shows a surprise. A price break above 0.15p would be a clear trigger.
playbook.if_passing: No terminal risk has been identified, so the position remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IG has plunged deep, sold a surplus warehouse and cut capital – a textbook setup for a mean‑reversion bounce. If the price recovers to halfway levels, upside could exceed 150 % while the downside is capped at the 70 % drawdown already experienced.

Historical data shows 3 rallies averaging 201% upside. Current position: -39.4%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
